name: Asset Acquisition
description: Capture or acquire heritage monument assets and prepare them for photogrammetry reconstruction.

steps.title: Plan the Acquisition Workflow
steps.description: Prepare the monument capture process and select the acquisition method before collecting data.
instructions: Assess the monument site for accessibility, lighting consistency, crowd movement, and physical obstacles.
For photogrammetry, prefer diffuse daylight or evenly lit conditions. Avoid harsh shadows, reflective surfaces, or rapidly changing lighting.
Decide whether the asset will be reconstructed using photogrammetry or LiDAR scanning depending on available equipment and required accuracy.
Prepare the required hardware such as a DSLR or mirrorless camera, tripod, spare batteries, storage media, and optional color checker.
Plan a complete coverage path around the monument before beginning image capture.
Launch Meshroom to verify that the reconstruction environment is installed and functioning correctly.

steps.title: Capture Photogrammetry Images
steps.description: Capture a complete and overlapping photo set suitable for high-quality 3D reconstruction.
instructions: Walk around the monument in multiple circular passes while maintaining a consistent distance from the surface.
Maintain approximately 60–80% overlap between adjacent photographs so Meshroom can reliably detect matching feature points.
Capture at least three vertical image rings: low-angle, eye-level, and elevated-angle coverage.
Take additional close-up detail photographs of carvings, inscriptions, or intricate architectural regions.
Avoid motion blur by using stable camera movement and sufficiently fast shutter speeds.
Avoid reflective, transparent, or moving objects whenever possible, since these reduce reconstruction accuracy.
Transfer all captured images into the project capture folder before beginning reconstruction.
checklist: Images reviewed for blur or exposure issues

steps.title: Reconstruct the 3D Asset in Meshroom
steps.description: Generate a textured 3D mesh from the captured image dataset using Meshroom photogrammetry.
instructions: Launch Meshroom and import the complete image dataset from the capture folder.
Allow Meshroom to detect feature points, match images, estimate camera positions, and generate the reconstruction graph.
Run the default reconstruction pipeline unless the dataset requires advanced parameter tuning.
Inspect the sparse and dense reconstruction stages for alignment issues or missing geometry.
After reconstruction completes, review the generated mesh for holes, floating artifacts, or incomplete surfaces.
Export the reconstructed model as OBJ or FBX along with its associated textures.
Store exported meshes and texture atlases inside the workflow output folder for later optimization in Blender.
checklist: Files transferred to workflow output folder
